title: calamagrostis purpurea subsp. phragmitoides
intro: Calamagrostis purpurea subsp. phragmitoides, also known as purple meadow-grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Calamagrostis purpurea subsp. phragmitoides include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
